question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that node.streamBlocks() is called with nextBlockNumber.get() after it may have been updated by hasBlock(), and if the attacker controls a node that sets nextBlockNumber to a value where shouldGetBlock() would return true but the actual block at that number contains manipulated fee data, cause the subscriber to process fee-corrupted blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEndOfBlock() in BlockAssembler] Can an unprivileged external user send an EndOfBlock message for a block number that was never started (pending.isEmpty()), and because onEndOfBlock() calls Utility.handleRecoverableError() (non-fatal) and returns without clearing state, cause subsequent legitimate blocks to be assembled with stale pending data containing manipulated fee information?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that creates a registered node with a domain name containing special characters or whitespace that, after trimming in extractHost(), produces a hostname matching a rogue server, causing the discovery service to add a rogue block node that serves blocks with manipulated transactionFeeCharged values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamBlocks() in BlockNode] Can an unprivileged external user operate a block node that sends a very large maxStreamResponseSize-sized response (up to 36MB) containing a single block with thousands of transactions all having manipulated congestionPricingMultiplier values, exploiting the lack of per-transaction fee validation in the stream processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: tryReadmit() in BlockNode] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitTime check in tryReadmit(), where a node becomes active again after readmitDelay (default 1 minute), to time an attack where a rogue node is temporarily deactivated to appear legitimate, then reactivated at a precise moment to serve a block with manipulated fee data during a window when legitimate nodes are also inactive?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause all legitimate block nodes to throw exceptions during getBlockRange() (e.g., by network disruption), causing getNode() to throw BlockStreamException('No block node can provide block'), and then register a rogue node that is the only one returning a valid block range, forcing the subscriber to use it and accept blocks with manipulated assessedCustomFees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: onBlockItemSet() in BlockAssembler] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockItemSet where the first item has ItemCase BLOCK_HEADER but subsequent items in the same set contain transaction results with negative transactionFeeCharged values (since protobuf int64 allows negatives), and because there is no sign validation, cause the mirror node to record negative fees that corrupt fee accounting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamBlocks() in BlockNode] Can an unprivileged external user operate a block node that sends BLOCK_ITEMS responses with an empty blockItems list, triggering the 'Received empty BlockItemSet' warning log but no error, and then immediately send a valid-looking block with manipulated highVolumePricingMultiplier, exploiting the non-fatal handling to inject fee-corrupted data after a benign-looking empty message?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that auto-discovered nodes (from the database) override configured nodes when they share the same getMergeKey(), by submitting a transaction that creates a registered node with the same host:port as a configured legitimate node but with different (attacker-controlled) properties, causing the subscriber to connect to a rogue endpoint serving fee-manipulated blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when EARLIEST_AVAILABLE_BLOCK_NUMBER is passed to doGet(), the shouldGetBlock() check uses the updated nextBlockNumber (set by hasBlock()), and if a rogue node sets this to a block number beyond endBlockNumber, cause the subscriber to silently skip processing the earliest available block, potentially missing fee records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEndOfBlock() in BlockAssembler] Can an unprivileged external user send a stream where the BlockHeader in the first pending item has a block number that differs from the EndOfBlock blockNumber, and because the mismatch is handled by Utility.handleRecoverableError() (non-fatal), cause the block to be assembled and processed with the wrong block number, leading to hash chain verification using incorrect block metadata while fee data from the manipulated block is persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AtomicReference cache in BlockNodeDiscoveryService, where cache.updateAndGet() is called with a lambda that checks if the list is the CLEARED sentinel, to cause a race condition where two threads simultaneously see CLEARED and both rebuild the node list, potentially including a rogue node in one rebuild that gets committed, causing fee-manipulated blocks to be served?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamBlocks() in BlockNode] Can an unprivileged external user operate a block node that deliberately delays responses to approach the responseTimeout (400ms default), causing the timeout() method in BlockAssembler to return near-zero or negative values, and exploiting any timeout handling edge cases to cause the subscriber to accept partially assembled blocks with incomplete or manipulated fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user register a block node with priority=-1 (below the default 0), and because BlockNodeProperties.priority has @Min(0) validation but this is only enforced at configuration load time, exploit a path where a discovered node bypasses validation and sorts before all configured nodes, causing the subscriber to always select the rogue node serving fee-manipulated blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: onBlockItemSet() in BlockAssembler] Can an unprivileged external user send a block stream where a BlockItemSet contains a transaction result with a transactionFeeCharged value of Long.MAX_VALUE (9223372036854775807 tinybars), and because there is no upper bound validation in the transformer pipeline, cause integer overflow or incorrect fee calculations when the mirror node processes or aggregates these fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that toBlockNodeProperties() only checks that a registered node's endpoint has all three TIER_ONE_BLOCK_NODE_APIS (STATUS, PUBLISH, SUBSCRIBE_STREAM), without verifying the node's identity or authenticity, to register a rogue node that passes all API checks but serves blocks with manipulated congestionPricingMultiplier values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamBlocks() in BlockNode] Can an unprivileged external user operate a block node that sends a SubscribeStreamResponse with ResponseCase.STATUS containing a non-SUCCESS code (e.g., READ_STREAM_INVALID_START_BLOCK_NUMBER), causing a BlockStreamException to be thrown, and by repeatedly triggering this for all legitimate nodes, force the subscriber to use a rogue node that serves blocks with manipulated transferList fee distributions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the log statement 'Start streaming block {} from {}' reveals which block node is being used, and by monitoring these logs (if accessible), determine when a legitimate node is being used versus when a rogue node has been selected, to time fee manipulation attacks precisely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: append() in BlockAssembler] Can an unprivileged external user send a block stream where a new BLOCK_HEADER arrives while pending is non-empty (previous block not yet completed), causing append() to throw BlockStreamException('Received block items of a new block while the previous block is still pending'), and by repeatedly triggering this, exhaust the error budget (maxSubscribeAttempts) of legitimate nodes, leaving only a rogue node to serve fee-manipulated blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlockRange() in BlockNode] Can an unprivileged external user operate a block node that returns a closed range [N, N] for a specific block N containing manipulated fee data, causing hasBlock() to return true only for that specific block, and by advertising this range only when the subscriber is looking for block N, ensure the rogue node is selected precisely when the fee-manipulated block is needed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the discovery service merges configured and discovered nodes using a HashMap (configurationsMap), where the last entry for a given getMergeKey() wins, and because discovered nodes are iterated after configured nodes in Iterables.concat(), register a discovered node with the same endpoint as a configured node to override it with a rogue node serving fee-manipulated blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: onBlockItemSet() in BlockAssembler] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockItemSet where the transaction result contains a transferList with transfers that don't sum to zero (violating conservation of value), and because the BlockAssembler performs no transfer list validation, cause the mirror node to persist an inconsistent fee transfer record that shows fees being created or destroyed outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamBlocks() in BlockNode] Can an unprivileged external user operate a block node that sends a grpcCall.read() that returns null (end of stream) immediately after sending one block with manipulated fee data, causing the while loop to exit normally without triggering onError(), so the tampered fee data is accepted as a complete and valid stream?
